shortPrayer.es: San Agustï¿½n de Hipona, Doctor de la Gracia, intercede por nosotros.
shortPrayer.en: Saint Augustine of Hippo, Doctor of Grace, intercede for us.

history.es:
San Agustin es el mayor teï¿½logo de Occidente y el santo de la conversion imposible: un hombre que vivio diecisiete anos en el pecado, cuya madre lloro por el durante decadas y que Dios llamo en el momento en que menos lo esperaba.

1. ï¿½TARDE TE AMEï¿½ ï¿½ LA GRAN CONVERSION DE MILAN (386 d.C.)
En el jardin de Milan, Agustin escucho la voz de un nino que cantaba repetidamente: ï¿½Toma y lee, toma y leeï¿½. Abrio las cartas de Pablo y sus ojos cayeron en Romanos 13:13-14: ï¿½No en comilonas y embriagueces... sino revestios del Senor Jesucristoï¿½. Mas tarde escribio: ï¿½No quise leer mas ni habia necesidad: al instante, al terminar esa frase, como si una luz de certeza se hubiera vertido en mi corazon, se disiparon todas las tinieblas de la dudaï¿½. Tenia 32 anos. Habia pasado diecisiete anos persiguiendo la verdad en el placer, en la filosofia maniquea, en el escepticismo. En ese instante, todo ceso. Su madre Monica, al saberlo, derramo lagrimas de gozo que eran el fruto de treinta anos de oraciones incansables.

2. LA VISION DEL NINO EN LA PLAYA Y EL MISTERIO DE LA TRINIDAD
Segun la tradicion, Agustin paseaba por la orilla del mar mientras meditaba el misterio de la Trinidad Santisima para su tratado ï¿½De Trinitateï¿½. Vio a un nino que habia hecho un hoyo en la arena y corria con una conchita al mar, traï¿½a agua y la vaciaba en el hoyo. ï¿½ï¿½Que haces?ï¿½, le pregunto Agustin. ï¿½Quiero meter todo el mar en este hoyoï¿½, respondio el nino. ï¿½Pero es imposibleï¿½, dijo el obispo. El nino lo miro y le dijo: ï¿½Y tu pretendes comprender el misterio de la Trinidad Santisima con tu pequeï¿½o entendimiento humanoï¿½. Cuando Agustin quiso responder, el nino habia desaparecido. Agustin se postro en la arena y lloro. La escena ha sido considerada por los teologos como una de las mas hermosas teofanias de la tradicion cristiana.

3. LA MUERTE ENTRE LAS MURALLAS Y EL LEGADO ETERNO (430 d.C.)
Cuando los vandalos asediaban Hipona, la ciudad de la que era obispo, Agustin no huyo. Se encerro con sus sacerdotes y paso los ultimos noventa dias de su vida en ayuno, oracion y lagrimas, rezando los Salmos penitenciales que habia mandado escribir en las paredes de su celda. Murio el 28 de agosto de 430, a los 75 anos, mientras la ciudad ardia. Pero su legado era imperecedero: 232 libros, 500 cartas, 500 sermones. La ï¿½Ciudad de Diosï¿½ y las ï¿½Confesionesï¿½ siguen siendo hoy los libros no bï¿½blicos mas leidos de la historia del cristianismo. Un filosofo pagano y lujurioso se habia convertido en el pilar intelectual de toda la civilizacion occidental.
history.en:
Saint Augustine is the greatest theologian of the West and the saint of the impossible conversion: a man who lived seventeen years in sin, whose mother wept for him for decades, and whom God called at the moment he least expected it.

1. "LATE HAVE I LOVED YOU" ï¿½ THE GREAT CONVERSION IN MILAN (386 AD)
In a garden in Milan, Augustine heard the voice of a child singing repeatedly: "Take and read, take and read." He opened Paul's letters and his eyes fell on Romans 13:13-14: "Not in carousing and drunkenness... but put on the Lord Jesus Christ." He later wrote: "I had no wish to read more and no need to do so. At that instant, as I reached the end of that sentence, a light of certainty flooded my heart and all the darkness of doubt was dispelled." He was 32 years old. He had spent seventeen years pursuing truth in pleasure, in Manichaean philosophy, in skepticism. In that instant, everything ceased. His mother Monica, upon hearing the news, wept with a joy that was the fruit of thirty years of tireless prayer.

2. THE VISION OF THE CHILD ON THE BEACH AND THE MYSTERY OF THE TRINITY
According to tradition, Augustine was walking along the seashore meditating on the mystery of the Most Holy Trinity for his treatise "De Trinitate." He saw a child who had dug a hole in the sand and was running with a little shell to the sea, bringing water and pouring it into the hole. "What are you doing?" Augustine asked. "I want to put the whole sea into this hole," answered the child. "But that is impossible," said the bishop. The child looked at him and said: "And you presume to comprehend the mystery of the Most Holy Trinity with your small human understanding." When Augustine tried to respond, the child had vanished. Augustine prostrated himself on the sand and wept. The scene has been regarded by theologians as one of the most beautiful theophanies in Christian tradition.

3. DEATH WITHIN THE WALLS AND THE ETERNAL LEGACY (430 AD)
When the Vandals were besieging Hippo, the city of which he was bishop, Augustine did not flee. He shut himself in with his priests and spent the last ninety days of his life in fasting, prayer, and tears, reciting the penitential Psalms he had ordered written on the walls of his cell. He died on August 28, 430, at the age of 75, while the city burned. But his legacy was imperishable: 232 books, 500 letters, 500 sermons. "The City of God" and the "Confessions" remain to this day the most widely read non-biblical books in the history of Christianity. A pagan, lustful philosopher had become the intellectual pillar of all Western civilization.

title.es: Novena a San Agustï¿½n de Hipona
title.en: Novena to Saint Augustine of Hippo

intention.es: Por la conversiï¿½n de los que se han alejado
intention.en: For the conversion of those who have strayed

prayer.es: San Agustï¿½n, que conociste el poder de la gracia de Dios para transformar el corazï¿½n mï¿½s extraviado, intercede por todos los que viven lejos de Dios. Que encuentren, como tï¿½, que el corazï¿½n estï¿½ inquieto hasta que descansa en ï¿½l. Amï¿½n.
prayer.en: Saint Augustine, who knew the power of God's grace to transform the most wayward heart, intercede for all who live far from God. May they find, as you did, that our heart is restless until it rests in Him. Amen.

intention.es: Por el amor a la verdad

prayer.es: Doctor de la Gracia, que buscaste la verdad con toda la fuerza de tu inteligencia y la encontraste en Cristo, intercede por todos los que buscan el sentido de la vida para que sean conducidos a la Verdad que es Dios. Amï¿½n.
prayer.en: Doctor of Grace, who sought truth with all the force of your intellect and found it in Christ, intercede for all who seek life's meaning that they may be led to the Truth who is God. Amen.

prayer.es: San Agustï¿½n, patrono de los teï¿½logos y filï¿½sofos, intercede para que todos los que cultivan la inteligencia la pongan al servicio de la fe y nunca la contrapongan al amor de Dios. Amï¿½n.
prayer.en: Saint Augustine, patron of theologians and philosophers, intercede that all who cultivate the intellect may put it at the service of faith and never pit it against the love of God. Amen.

intention.es: Por el don de la gracia

prayer.es: Agustï¿½n, Doctor de la Gracia, que comprendiste que sin Dios nada podemos hacer, intercede para que reconozcamos nuestra necesidad de la gracia divina y nos abramos plenamente a su acciï¿½n en nosotros. Amï¿½n.
prayer.en: Augustine, Doctor of Grace, who understood that without God we can do nothing, intercede that we may recognize our need for divine grace and fully open ourselves to its action in us. Amen.

prayer.es: San Agustï¿½n, que diste tu vida como pastor de la Iglesia de Hipona y la defendiste con tu pluma y tu voz, intercede por la Iglesia universal, especialmente en sus momentos de crisis y persecuciï¿½n. Amï¿½n.
prayer.en: Saint Augustine, who gave your life as pastor of the Church of Hippo and defended her with your pen and voice, intercede for the universal Church, especially in her moments of crisis and persecution. Amen.

intention.es: Por los que dudan en la fe
intention.en: For those who doubt in faith

prayer.es: Agustï¿½n, que pasaste por largos aï¿½os de duda y bï¿½squeda, intercede por todos los que atraviesan crisis de fe para que no se rindan sino que, como tï¿½, sigan buscando hasta encontrar la luz. Amï¿½n.
prayer.en: Augustine, who spent long years of doubt and searching, intercede for all going through faith crises that they may not give up but, like you, keep seeking until they find the light. Amen.

prayer.es: San Agustï¿½n, que experimentaste el poder esclavizante de los vicios y la libertad que trae la gracia, intercede por todos los que luchan contra sus pasiones para que encuentren en Dios la fuerza de la verdadera libertad. Amï¿½n.
prayer.en: Saint Augustine, who experienced the enslaving power of vices and the freedom grace brings, intercede for all who struggle against their passions that they may find in God the strength of true freedom. Amen.

intention.es: Por los que sufren el peso del pecado

prayer.es: Misericordioso Agustï¿½n, que experimentaste la infinita misericordia de Dios, proclama ante el trono divino la causa de todos los pecadores arrepentidos. Que nadie desespere de la misericordia de Dios. Amï¿½n.
prayer.en: Merciful Augustine, who experienced the infinite mercy of God, proclaim before the divine throne the cause of all repentant sinners. May no one despair of God's mercy. Amen.

prayer.es: San Agustï¿½n, que escribiste: 'Nos hiciste para Ti, Seï¿½or, y nuestro corazï¿½n estï¿½ inquieto hasta que descanse en Ti', ayï¿½danos a orientar toda nuestra vida hacia Dios, que es nuestro ï¿½nico descanso verdadero y nuestra patria definitiva. Amï¿½n.
prayer.en: Saint Augustine, who wrote: 'You made us for Yourself, O Lord, and our heart is restless until it rests in You', help us to direct our entire life toward God, who is our only true rest and our ultimate homeland. Amen.

title.es: Letanï¿½a de San Agustï¿½n de Hipona
title.en: Litany of Saint Augustine of Hippo

invocations.es: San Agustï¿½n, Doctor de la Gracia,
San Agustï¿½n, luz de la teologï¿½a occidental,
invocations.en: Saint Augustine, light of Western theology,

invocations.es: San Agustï¿½n, hijo agradecido de Santa Mï¿½nica,
invocations.en: Saint Augustine, grateful son of Saint Monica,

invocations.es: San Agustï¿½n, buscador incansable de la verdad,
invocations.en: Saint Augustine, tireless seeker of truth,

invocations.es: San Agustï¿½n, pastor solï¿½cito de Hipona,
invocations.en: Saint Augustine, solicitous pastor of Hippo,

invocations.es: San Agustï¿½n, defensor de la fe ortodoxa,
invocations.en: Saint Augustine, defender of orthodox faith,

invocations.es: San Agustï¿½n, corazï¿½n inquieto que descansï¿½ en Dios,
invocations.en: Saint Augustine, restless heart that found rest in God,
